choices: Hand-writing a novel for every column
Auto-generated training metadata in a structured documentation file
Deleting dataset versions after each epoch
items.explain: The example emits JSON fields such as dataset version, dates, transforms, and sources after training.

items.prompt: Example 8.43 embeds DVC stages so sensor ingest, cleaning, and training remain version-linked.
items.explain: Stage YAML ties pipeline commands and dependencies into an executable history.

items.prompt: Example 8.47 presents blockchain-style ledgers mainly as:
choices: A mandatory requirement for every student project
An emerging idea for stronger version auditability
A replacement for all data dictionaries
A way to avoid access control
items.explain: The chapter frames blockchain audit trails as an emerging trend to evaluate carefully.

items.prompt: Name two emerging trends mentioned besides blockchain-style auditability.
items.rubric: Any two of: AI-assisted documentation, real-time/streaming versioning, metadata standards, cloud/edge sync.
items.sample_answer: AI-assisted documentation generation and real-time versioning for streaming or edge data.

items.prompt: Lineage tracking can support compliance by documenting origins, transforms, and access over a dataset’s life.
items.explain: Advanced topics link lineage tools to audit trails under privacy regulations.
